authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis cultivator: self-inspection checklist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's official cultivator inspection checklist is a direct cultivation-operations reference for track-and-trace timing, premises documents, canopy limits, pesticide controls, water-source evidence, power-source controls, and record retention.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Site readiness, plant tracking, and inspection evidence review
used_for: premises, pest, waste, water, and power evidence expectations
record retention and inspection scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: Metrc
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Massachusetts CCC is the state cannabis regulator and its Metrc page explicitly states that the Commonwealth uses Metrc to track every plant from seed to sale and that all licensees must use it.
used_for: boundary against generic agriculture tooling assumptions

authoritative_sources.title: CTS for OLCC Marijuana Licensees
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OLCC's CTS guidance is authoritative because it states that Oregon's Cannabis Tracking System is integral to regulation, requires licensees and employees to use it, and ties access to regulator-approved training.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Track-and-trace access, training, and data-entry readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Sampling and Testing Metrc Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OLCC's official guide defines harvest lots, batches, Metrc test status behavior, reanalysis, retesting, remediation, and destruction routing in a way that directly constrains cultivation workflow claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Harvest, testing, remediation, and destruction handling
used_for: testing, reanalysis, retest, and remediation lifecycle steps
exception and completion boundaries tied to lab status

authoritative_sources.title: Information for new cannabis licence holders: Cultivation, processing or sale for medical purposes licences
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Health Canada is the federal cannabis licensing authority and this page is authoritative for post-licence operating obligations such as CRA licence dependencies, local authority notices, and annual regulatory responsibilities that affect cultivation readiness.
used_for: runtime requirement for federal-role and reporting context
adjacent authority boundaries around licensing administration

authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis organizational security plan
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Health Canada's OSP guidance is authoritative for diversion-prevention controls, required security-cleared roles, SOP consistency, master grower and QAP role context, and change-notification obligations.
used_for: escalation design for diversion and OSP inconsistency cases

authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis Tracking System: Monthly Reporting Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Health Canada's CTS reporting guide is authoritative for CTLS reporting access, monthly submission workflow, reporting accountability, inventory and sales reporting fields, and correction handling for federal licence holders.
used_for: record-of-truth expectations for inventory and reporting data

authoritative_sources.title: Good production practices guide for cannabis
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Health Canada's GPP guidance is authoritative for contamination controls, testing expectations, sample retention, validated methods, investigations, and record retention for federally licensed cannabis production.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Testing, quality evidence, and document-retention review

authoritative_sources.title: Reporting requirements for cannabis and industrial hemp licences
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Health Canada's reporting requirements page is authoritative for reportable test results, loss or theft reporting, and event-based reporting deadlines that materially affect cultivation exception handling.

workflow_stages.name: licence and site prerequisite confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, licence class, site, responsible roles, approved plans, security posture, and systems-of-record links before any cultivation execution state is changed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: plant inventory, canopy, and environmental execution handling
workflow_stages.description: Use plant tags, immature-lot records, canopy or room moves, environmental logs, pesticide and water evidence, and SOP context to decide whether cultivation work may proceed or must hold.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: harvest, testing, remediation, and disposition control
workflow_stages.description: Validate harvest batches, package identifiers, test status, remediation or retest posture, and destruction handling before harvested cannabis is treated as ready for downstream transfer or closure.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: completion, transfer readiness, and reporting closeout
workflow_stages.description: Require completion evidence such as package assignment, laboratory disposition, transfer or destruction records, monthly reporting posture, and resolved exceptions before closure.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: inspection, diversion, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when inspection findings, OSP inconsistencies, loss or theft events, missing permits, quality-release boundaries, or jurisdictional conflicts move authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: cultivation, nursery, or micro or standard licence records and site identifiers
premises diagram, cultivation plan, pest management plan, waste management plan, water-source records, and power-source records
organizational security plan, role maps, security-clearance records, SOP references, and master grower or QAP ownership notes
seed-to-sale records including plant tags, immature lots, canopy movements, harvest batches, package UID tags, transfer manifests, and destruction or disposal entries
environmental, irrigation, fertigation, pesticide, sanitation, and worker-safety logs tied to the affected site or room
testing samples, certificates of analysis, remediation or retest notes, and validated laboratory method references where applicable
inspection checklists, CAPA items, incident logs, loss or theft reports, and regulator correspondence
CTLS monthly reports, statement of cannabis revenue submissions, reporter-access records, and correction records
systems_of_record: licensing portal and Cannabis Tracking and Licensing System (CTLS)
seed-to-sale tracking system such as Metrc or regulator-mandated CTS
quality or laboratory information management system
document control repository for plans, SOPs, and security artifacts
tenant cultivation execution or crop-management log system

allowed: Assess whether visible cultivation records support advancing work, holding it, sending it to rework, or closing it with explicit evidence references.
Summarize matched and unmatched evidence, missing prerequisites, current lifecycle state, and the next responsible owner.
Separate licensing, seed-to-sale, testing, reporting, and diversion-control paths when the reviewed public baseline requires different evidence or approvals.
escalate: Jurisdiction, licence class, approved plans, or systems-of-record links are missing or conflict with the reviewed baseline.
Testing, remediation, environmental, security, inspection, or reporting evidence changes whether harvested material may proceed or close.
QAP, laboratory, compliance, security, transport, environmental, legal, or regulator-facing ownership is required.
refuse: Requests to alter plant, harvest, package, testing, or reporting facts without source evidence.
Requests to approve licences, permits, pesticide exceptions, water-source changes, quality release, remediation outcome, or transport authority.
Requests to treat the cannabis label as cosmetic and skip seed-to-sale, testing, security, or jurisdiction context.

domain_failure_modes: A harvest or transfer item is marked complete even though the package, test, or manifest records do not materially agree.
A generic agriculture workflow is applied without recognizing cannabis-specific controls such as plant tags, harvest batches, package UIDs, CTLS reporting, or diversion-sensitive security roles.
An unresolved loss or theft issue, inspection finding, or OSP inconsistency is suppressed to preserve throughput or crop schedule.
The lane drifts into QAP release, laboratory disposition, legal interpretation, security-plan approval, distribution authorization, or regulator-facing reporting authority.

State, provincial, territorial, or federal workflow differences are flattened into one universal cultivation rule set even though tagging, testing, reporting, and transport ownership differ materially.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific differences in track-and-trace timing, transport ownership, and inventory reconciliation cadence
unresolved_ambiguities.why_unresolved: California, Oregon, Massachusetts, and Health Canada use different operational models and reporting windows, and those differences can materially change the exact next step even when the high-level lifecycle is the same.
unresolved_ambiguities.runtime_requirement: Require tenant jurisdiction, applicable regulator, and current tenant-approved workflow or SOP set before issuing deterministic timing or transport recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific environmental control stacks and crop-steering tooling
unresolved_ambiguities.why_unresolved: Public sources establish record and control expectations but do not define each tenant's climate-control platform, irrigation logic, fertigation recipe, or seasonal escalation thresholds.
unresolved_ambiguities.runtime_requirement: Require the tenant's actual cultivation execution systems, sensor thresholds, and environmental escalation rules before any recommendation depends on those controls.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Role delegation differences for master grower, QAP, responsible person, and head of security
unresolved_ambiguities.why_unresolved: Public guidance names these roles, but the precise approval chain and delegated authority vary by jurisdiction and tenant operating model.
unresolved_ambiguities.runtime_requirement: Require the tenant's current authority matrix and role map before work crosses into approval, release, security, or regulator-notice boundaries.
